I am writing to express my profound concern and dissatisfaction regarding the data breach that occurred at American Express which compromised the personal information of millions, including myself. 

As a consumer, I trusted AMEX to securely handle my sensitive information. However, the breach has exposed me to potential risks, including identity theft and financial fraud. Disturbingly, I have discovered that my information is now circulating on the dark web, exacerbating my fears of misuse. 

This situation has significantly affected my mental well-being, causing considerable stress and anxiety over the potential misuse of my personal data. The uncertainty and vulnerability I feel are deeply unsettling, impacting both my personal and financial security. 

Given these circumstances, I request that AMEX provide clear information on the steps being taken to prevent such breaches in the future, as well as the measures in place to protect affected individuals. I also seek guidance on how to monitor my credit and protect myself from any misuse of my personal data. 

Furthermore, I urge AMEX to offer free credit monitoring services and identity theft protection for an extended period, en

Subject : Dispute of Collection Account Under 12 CFR 1006.34 ( d ) and 15 USC 1681s-2 ( a ) ( 7 ) ( A ) Dear CFPB, I am writing to formally dispute the collection account listed on my credit report by Midland Credit Management . This account is associated with account number Account Number : XXXX XXXXXXXXXXXX and is negatively impacting my credit score.

Upon reviewing my credit report, I have identified the following issues, which I believe warrant the removal of this collection account : Failure to Provide Required Information : Under 12 CFR 1006.34 ( d ), debt collectors are required to provide specific validation information when attempting to collect a debt. Midland Credit Management has failed to provide the necessary validation information, including purchase agreement, the name of the original creditor and itemization of the debt. 

Incorrect Reporting Practices : As per 15 USC 1681s-2 ( a ) ( 7 ) ( A ), furnishers of information to consumer reporting agencies are required to report accurate information. In this case, the information reported by Midland Credit Management is inaccurate because the debt is being reported without proper validation and it does not belong to me.

This account is associated with account number Account Number : XXXX XXXXXXXXXXXX and is negatively impacting my credit score.

Upon reviewing my credit report, I have identified the following issues, which I believe warrant the removal of this collection account : Failure to Provide Required Information : Under 12 CFR 1006.34 ( d ), debt collectors are required to provide specific validation information when attempting to collect a debt. To Whom It May Concern, I am writing to formally dispute the enforceability of my federal student loan agreement on the basis of a **material alteration to the original contract**. 

According to the terms outlined in my promissory note, my loan was issued and administered by the **U.S. Department of Education** . If such notice from the consumer is made by mail, notification shall be complete upon receipt.
